title: Chakravyuh
description: A maze-navigation robotics challenge where teams built bots to solve complex paths during the techno-cultural fest.
detailedDescription:
Chakravyuh is a maze-navigation robotics challenge conducted during the techno-cultural fest. Teams design autonomous bots to detect turns, avoid dead ends, and finish the route in minimum time.

What makes it special:
- Focus on sensing and autonomous decision-making
- Strong test of control logic in constrained paths
- Encourages rapid debugging and strategy tuning

title: Codejuring
description: An online event combining an aptitude round with a coding test to evaluate logic and programming speed.
detailedDescription:
Codejuring is an online challenge structured around aptitude and coding rounds. Participants are evaluated on logical reasoning, problem breakdown, and implementation speed under time pressure.

Event focus:
- Analytical thinking and coding fundamentals
- Fast decision-making with clean execution
- Beginner-friendly but highly competitive format

title: Freshers Orientation
description: An interactive onboarding session for freshers to introduce club activities, domains, and opportunities.
detailedDescription:
Freshers Orientation is the gateway event for new students to explore the Robotics Club ecosystem. It introduces domains, project culture, mentorship workflows, and participation opportunities.

Session outcomes:
- Clarity on club structure and tracks
- Better onboarding into technical teams
- Early engagement through interactive activities

title: Arduino Jam
description: A practical Arduino learning session covering IDE setup, sensor basics, and circuit simulation workflows.
detailedDescription:
Arduino Jam is a hands-on workshop built for quick entry into embedded systems. Participants learn setup, pin-level understanding, sensor interfacing, and simulation before hardware deployment.

Workshop scope:
- Arduino IDE and code upload pipeline
- Sensor integration basics
- Practical circuit-building and testing

title: Hack O Heist
description: A 36-hour themed hackathon focused on building practical solutions for social and ecological challenges.
detailedDescription:
Hack O Heist is a 36-hour themed hackathon where teams build rapid prototypes for real-world social and ecological problems. It promotes product thinking, teamwork, and fast delivery.

Core experience:
- Intense sprint-based development cycle
- Problem-first innovation mindset
- Pitch-ready outcomes in limited time

title: Robowar
description: A high-energy combat robotics showdown featuring custom-built bots in an arena battle format.
detailedDescription:
Robowar is a crowd-favorite combat robotics event where teams build battle bots and compete in controlled arena matches. Design strength, strategy, and driver control determine outcomes.

Competition focus:
- Mechanical robustness and bot survivability
- Tactical movement and arena positioning
- Fast-paced head-to-head engagement

title: Digital Detective
description: A 24-hour technical treasure hunt where participants solved encrypted riddles and logic clues.
detailedDescription:
Digital Detective is a 24-hour technical treasure hunt driven by ciphers, clues, and logic sequences. Participants solve progressive challenge layers to unlock the next stage.

Event strengths:
- Puzzle solving with technical context
- Time-bound collaborative problem solving
- High engagement for mixed skill groups

title: The Next Hokage
description: A Robo Race event where teams tested speed, control, and engineering reliability on competitive tracks.
detailedDescription:
The Next Hokage is a Robo Race format event built around speed and precision. Teams tune their robots for acceleration, handling, and consistency across race segments.

Race emphasis:
- Control stability at higher speeds
- Reliability under repeated runs
- Track strategy and tuning efficiency

title: RoboWar
description: A flagship battle-bot event where student teams designed and programmed robots for direct arena clashes.
detailedDescription:
RoboWar is a flagship battle-bot competition featuring direct arena clashes between student-built robots. Teams optimize drive systems, impact endurance, and tactical operation.

Key highlights:
- High-intensity competitive environment
- Integration of hardware and control strategy
- Strong public showcase of team engineering

title: Abhiyutthan
description: A multi-event technical showcase designed to engage students through fun, skill-based robotics challenges.
detailedDescription:
Abhiyutthan is a multi-event technical series aimed at broad student participation. It combines fun and learning through structured robotics challenges for varied experience levels.

Why it stands out:
- Multiple challenge formats in one event cycle
- Inclusive participation model
- Strong blend of engagement and technical growth

title: Machine Mayhem
description: A bot-track challenge where participants designed running bots and competed on a defined obstacle route.
detailedDescription:
Machine Mayhem is a bot-track challenge where participants design and run robots through constrained obstacle paths. It tests build quality, drive control, and maneuver planning.

Event goals:
- Practical bot fabrication exposure
- Control refinement in obstacle conditions
- Performance benchmarking across teams

title: Soccer Bot Challenge
description: A football-themed robotics event combining sports strategy with bot design and control.
detailedDescription:
Soccer Bot Challenge combines robotics with football-style gameplay. Teams develop bots capable of coordinated movement, ball control, and strategic play in competitive rounds.

Challenge dimensions:
- Motion control and directional precision
- Team strategy in dynamic scenarios
- Fast adaptation during match play

title: Laser Bot Race
description: An innovation-driven racing event where teams built and tuned bots for speed and precision performance.
detailedDescription:
Laser Bot Race is an innovation-led speed challenge where teams optimize their bots for precision runs and stable high-speed movement. The event rewards both design quality and execution discipline.

Focus areas:
- Speed control with reduced drift
- Track-based tuning and calibration
- Consistent lap performance under pressure

title: Drone Workshop
description: A hands-on workshop introducing drone components, flight basics, safety protocols, and practical setup for beginners.
detailedDescription:
Drone Workshop is a practical learning session designed to introduce UAV architecture and safe flight practices. Participants explore key components, basic control logic, and setup workflows.

Workshop outcomes:
- Understanding of core drone subsystems
- Exposure to flight safety and calibration
- Better readiness for advanced aerial robotics projects
